description_long: This is a compact, single-story bungalow with a boxy, low-profile form and a flat or gently sloped roof. The exterior features a mix of light beige and brown tones, with large rectangular windows distributed across several facades, providing ample natural light. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from wood and concrete, with some metallic and glass elements visible in the window frames and doors. The building's layout is irregular, with several protruding sections and a possible small utility area or side entrance. There is no visible porch, but the windows are arranged in an asymmetrical pattern, some grouped together and others spaced apart. The overall style suggests a modern or mid-century urban residential design, suitable for a suburban or city neighborhood.
description: A modern, single-story bungalow with a flat roof, beige and brown exterior, and large asymmetrical windows. The structure is boxy and compact, with several protruding sections and no visible porch.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main facade with large windows and a prominent brown section, showing the building's irregular, boxy layout.
description_view_1: From this angle, the flat roof and the arrangement of windows along the side are visible, with more emphasis on the building's depth and rear sections.
description_view_2: This perspective shows the back and side of the bungalow, revealing additional windows and the overall compactness of the structure.
description_view_3: This view provides a look at the side and partial interior, with visible room divisions and a utility area, emphasizing the building's asymmetrical design.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and spaced windows
